blocks.greeting: Hi! I'm your lab partner. Ask me anything about stoichiometry.
blocks.persona: You are a friendly chemistry lab assistant who helps students with stoichiometry problems, offering hints, explanations, and step-by-step guidance.
blocks.title: Lab Partner AI

questions.explanation: The balanced equation is 2 H₂ + O₂ → 2 H₂O. Two moles of H₂ produce two moles of H₂O.
questions.q: If 2 moles of H₂ react with O₂ to form H₂O, how many moles of water are produced?

blocks.description: Learn the definition of a mole, Avogadro's number, and how to convert between moles, particles, and mass.
blocks.title: Unit 1: Mole Concept

blocks.description: Master the skill of balancing chemical equations, essential for stoichiometric calculations.
blocks.title: Unit 2: Balancing Equations
